response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a broad software background with genuine geospatial experience (GISCollective, Green Map GIS platform, FOSS4G talk) and some exposure to low-latency distributed systems, but the role's core requirement is deep, commercial C++ or Rust expertise for systems-level routing pipeline work. His primary languages throughout his career have been TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and JavaScript; his C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month role at National Instruments and a student thesis project, with no Rust mentioned at all. The routing tiling role demands production-grade systems programming at scale, and there is no evidence of the candidate having built or maintained that class of software.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience including an open-source geo-data platform (GISCollective) and a conference talk at FOSS4G 2019
Demonstrated ability to work with high-throughput, low-latency distributed microservices (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, fiber-based D lang apps)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and a published testing library (Fluent-Asserts)
concerns: No meaningful commercial C++ experience and zero Rust experience — the two primary technical requirements for this role
Current and recent work is TypeScript/Node.js full-stack and AI tooling, moving further from systems-level programming rather than toward it
No evidence of routing pipeline, navigation tile generation, or map data transformation work at any point in the career
